commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainers should decide the permanent WhatsApp inbound contract before repair, because the fix could be socket emission, webhook replay, or a narrower documented scope.; Needs attention: The diff introduces an unauthenticated admin inbound endpoint for WhatsApp fake-provider message injection.; Review finding: [P1] Wire inbound messages into the WhatsApp controller — src/fake-servers/whatsapp.ts:542 - [P2]... (sha=c3549fb057c60f4d9c340286bf8df1b092365eff)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a WhatsApp fake provider server, Baileys-shaped mock/controller exports, CLI `serve whatsapp` support, OpenClaw bridge wiring, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: subscribe to the mock socket, POST to the WhatsApp admin inbound URL, and observe that the handler only returns JSON while socket events are emitted only from outbound `sendMessage()`. I did not execute tests because this review must keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers should decide the permanent WhatsApp inbound contract before repair, because the fix could be socket emission, webhook replay, or a narrower documented scope.

target.title: Reject split outbound agent session ownership
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96075.

Outbound send/action paths could accept an explicit `agentId` while also carrying an agent-scoped session key owned by another agent. That could split ownership across media policy, plugin dispatch, transcript mirroring, queueing, and internal hook correlation.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a shared outbound ownership guard that parses `agent:<owner>:...` session keys and compares only the normalized owner segment against explicit agent owners. The guard is applied at both Gateway boundaries and lower outbound helpers so internal callers cannot bypass the Gateway check.

`policyKey` remains intentionally excluded from equality checks because routed ACP-style delivery can use a separate policy/origin session while the canonical session/mirror keys still describe the owning agent.

## User Impact

Contradictory outbound requests such as `agentId: "work"` with `sessionKey: "agent:main:..."` now fail before dispatch, media resolution, durable queueing, mirroring, or hook correlation. Omitted/blank agent ids, legacy session keys, same-agent different conversation keys, and cross-agent delivery policy keys remain supported.

## Compatibility Note

This intentionally changes contradictory split-owner outbound requests from accepted dispatch to fail-closed validation. The rejected shape is an explicit agent owner paired with an agent-scoped session key owned by a different agent, for example `agentId: "work"` with `sessionKey: "agent:main:slack:channel:c1"`.

I am not preserving explicit-agent precedence for that contradictory shape because it can route media policy, plugin dispatch, transcript mirroring, queueing, and hook correlation under different owners. Same-agent session keys, omitted or blank agent ids, legacy/non-agent session keys, and cross-agent `policyKey` routing remain allowed.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

Ran locally against `0b58b0592049` with a one-off `node --import tsx` outbound-helper proof that calls the real `deliverOutboundPayloads()` path with a local Matrix outbound adapter. The mismatch case rejects before the adapter is called; the allowed cases each reach the adapter.

```text
{"case":"mismatch explicit agentId plus agent session key","status":"rejected","adapterCalls":0,"error":"deliverOutboundPayloads agentId \"work\" does not match session key agent \"main\"","session":{"agentId":"work","key":"agent:main:matrix:room:ops"}}
{"case":"same agent different conversation key","status":"allowed","adapterCalls":1,"results":[{"channel":"matrix","messageId":"proof-1"}],"session":{"agentId":"work","key":"agent:work:matrix:room:ops"}}
{"case":"legacy non-agent session key","status":"allowed","adapterCalls":1,"results":[{"channel":"matrix","messageId":"proof-2"}],"session":{"agentId":"work","key":"legacy-session"}}
{"case":"cross-agent policyKey excluded","status":"allowed","adapterCalls":1,"results":[{"channel":"matrix","messageId":"proof-3"}],"session":{"agentId":"claude","key":"agent:claude:acp:spawned","policyKey":"agent:main:main"}}
```

### Tests and checks

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - Passed 2 Vitest shards: 2 gateway test files / 122 tests, then 5 infra test files / 195 tests.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts src/infra/outbound/session-owner.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Review the outbound owner-mismatch guard changes. Focus on whether the shared helper correctly compares explicit agent ownership against agent-scoped session-key owners, whether policyKey remains intentionally excluded, whether Gateway send/message.action and lower outbound helpers reject before dispatch/queue/mirroring, and whether tests cover same-agent, legacy, mismatch, and cross-agent policyKey cases.'`
target.author: youngting520
